# Discord Channel Analysis: 🥇-partners

## 1. Summary

This brief chat segment primarily focused on two topics: elizaOS token holder benefits and the milady.ai token launch status.

Odilitime shared news that elizaOS holders will receive an upgrade, linking to a related announcement. DannyNOR NoFapArc inquired about purchasing the milady.ai token through Phantom wallet but couldn't locate it. Odilitime clarified that the token hasn't launched yet and committed to following up for more information.

The conversation also touched on creative collaboration strategy. Odilitime mentioned the team is expanding partnerships with external artists who share similar values and goals, moving beyond just internal labs talent. This approach was described as successful ("paying off"). The recovery of X (Twitter) accounts was noted as beneficial for these collaborative efforts.

No technical implementations, code discussions, or specific problem-solving occurred in this segment. The chat was primarily informational updates and brief community questions.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Where do we buy the milady.ai token, can't find it in phantom? (asked by DannyNOR NoFapArc) A: I don't think it's launched yet, I'll ask (answered by Odilitime)

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: DannyNOR NoFapArc | Context: Unable to find milady.ai token in Phantom wallet | Resolution: Clarified token hasn't launched yet and offered to investigate further

## 4. Action Items

Type: Technical | Description: Follow up on milady.ai token launch status and availability | Mentioned By: Odilitime

# Discord Channel Analysis: 💬-coders

## 1. Summary

This chat segment contains minimal technical discussion, primarily consisting of announcements and brief observations. The most significant technical contribution came from Ogie, who announced the publication of a SolCex Exchange plugin for elizaOS (v2.0.0). This plugin, available as @buzzbd/plugin-solcex-bd on npm, functions as an autonomous business development agent specifically designed for cryptocurrency exchange listings. The plugin integrates 8 services, implements a 100-point token scoring system, and aggregates data from 16 intelligence sources. Ogie submitted a registry pull request (#263) and referenced ERC-8004 implementations on both Base (#17483) and Ethereum (#25045) networks. The developer expressed interest in collaborating with builders working on Jeju or agent commerce projects.

Jin made a brief observation about the elizaOS news RSS feed, sharing the feed URL (https://elizaos.news/rss/feed.xml). Odilitime provided a minor correction regarding the project naming convention, clarifying that the correct capitalization is "elizaOS" rather than "ElizaOS." Matt requested assistance with opening a ticket but received no visible response in this segment.

The chat shows limited collaborative problem-solving or in-depth technical discussions during this timeframe.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Can I get assistance with opening a ticket? (asked by Matt - Timpi) A: Unanswered

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: jin | Context: Incorrect capitalization of project name | Resolution: Corrected naming convention from "ElizaOS" to "elizaOS"

## 4. Action Items

Type: Feature | Description: Review and merge registry PR #263 for SolCex Exchange plugin | Mentioned By: Ogie
Type: Technical | Description: Connect with builders working on Jeju/agent commerce for potential collaboration on SolCex plugin | Mentioned By: Ogie
Type: Technical | Description: Investigate ticket opening assistance request | Mentioned By: Matt - Timpi

# Discord Chat Analysis - core-devs Channel

## 1. Summary

The discussion centered around PR #6521 for elizaOS/eliza, which involves database refactoring and migration strategy changes for version 2.0.0. Stan submitted a PR with significant code deletion (-2600 lines) primarily removing auto-migration functionality from version 1.4.x to 1.6.x, as the team decided this migration path is no longer necessary for v2.0.0. The rationale is that users are assumed to not be on 1.4.x anymore, with a potential future implementation to throw an error if direct migration from 1.4.x to v2.0.0 is attempted, directing users to migrate to 1.6.x first.

Odilitime mentioned working on a separate "great db refactor PR" and expressed interest in integrating Stan's changes, noting he had ensured Row Level Security (RLS) was maintained in his refactor. Stan confirmed the changes are minimal and easy to rebase, making integration straightforward.

A brief discussion occurred about HTTP server implementation wrapping runtime in V2.0.0. Stan inquired if Odilitime had added this functionality, which Odilitime denied, but Stan later confirmed that Milady has this implementation along with numerous examples in the 2.0.0 version.

Odilitime reported encountering 200k token limits regularly due to numerous plugins, identifying bootstrap providers and evaluations as primary contributors to the issue. He is actively working on improvements to address these baseline performance concerns.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Why was the auto migration from 1.4.x to 1.6.x removed in v2? (asked by Odilitime) A: Because the team assumes users are no longer on version 1.4.x, and they could potentially throw an error asking users to migrate to 1.6.x first before going to v2.0.0 (answered by Stan ⚡)

Q: Did you add a small HTTP server that wraps runtime on V2.0.0? (asked by Stan ⚡) A: No, but Milady has it and there are examples in 2.0.0 (answered by Odilitime)

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Stan ⚡ | Helpee: Odilitime | Context: Concerns about rebasing changes from PR #6521 with the db refactor PR | Resolution: Stan confirmed the changes are small and very easy to rebase, with the big deletion being migration-related code that's no longer needed

Helper: Stan ⚡ | Helpee: Odilitime | Context: Understanding why auto-migration was removed | Resolution: Explained the migration strategy change and suggested potential error handling for direct 1.4.x to v2.0.0 upgrades

## 4. Action Items

Type: Technical | Description: Rebase and deconflict PR #6521 with the db refactor PR | Mentioned By: Odilitime

Type: Technical | Description: Consider implementing error handling to prevent direct migration from 1.4.x to v2.0.0, directing users to 1.6.x first | Mentioned By: Stan ⚡

Type: Technical | Description: Improve bootstrap providers and evaluations to address 200k token limit issues caused by numerous plugins | Mentioned By: Odilitime

# Discord Chat Analysis for 💬-discussion

## 1. Summary

The discussion centered around ElizaOS development capabilities and ecosystem updates. DarmaStef showcased experience building AI agents using ElizaOS, including Reservation Agent, Travel Agent, and Database analysis Agent for non-technical users, offering to collaborate on new projects.

A significant announcement came from Odilitime regarding an upgrade for ElizaOS holders, referencing a tweet from shawmakesmagic. The conversation touched on market capitalization history, with the token previously reaching almost 3B according to DannyNOR NoFapArc.

Technical discussions focused on NFT capabilities and infrastructure. Odilitime mentioned ongoing plugin-evm work that will unlock more NFT functionality, acknowledging current limitations. A question arose about Spartan infrastructure routing Milady wallet orders. Odilitime clarified that while Milady wallet might use similar tech/plugins as Spartan, it's not directly related to their implementation.

There was confusion about a Milady BSC announcement, with user 'g' questioning whether an upgrade was for the wrong token ($milady.ai vs ElizaOS). The team's activity status was questioned by albi06856, with Odilitime confirming continued active development.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Can you create any AI agent with any capabilities via ElizaOS? (asked by Omid Sa) A: Implied yes, as DarmaStef demonstrated building various agent types (answered by Omid Sa)

Q: What was the top market cap of this? (asked by Alexei) A: Almost 3B (answered by DannyNOR NoFapArc)

Q: Is this team still active? (asked by albi06856) A: Yes sir (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Could this run again? (asked by albi06856) A: Unanswered

Q: Is there an Eliza NFT? (asked by brickolascage) A: Maybe they'll do an elizaOK one or something (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Is Spartan set up to route and execute Milady wallet orders? (asked by Skinny) A: Might be using the same tech/plugins, so it's spartan tech but not really related to our dude (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Can price be discussed here? (asked by al.mark) A: Discuss in the trading channel (answered by MDMnvest)

Q: Isn't the upgrade for $milady.ai not ElizaOS? (asked by g) A: Unanswered

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Omid Sa | Helpee: General audience | Context: Demonstrating ElizaOS capabilities | Resolution: Suggested asking DarmaStef to create AI agents with any capabilities via ElizaOS

Helper: DannyNOR NoFapArc | Helpee: Alexei | Context: Question about historical market cap | Resolution: Provided specific figure of almost 3B

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: albi06856 | Context: Concern about team activity status | Resolution: Confirmed team is still active

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: Skinny | Context: Confusion about Spartan and Milady wallet infrastructure relationship | Resolution: Clarified they may use same tech/plugins but aren't directly related

Helper: MDMnvest | Helpee: al.mark | Context: Uncertainty about where to discuss price | Resolution: Directed to appropriate trading channel

Helper: Lexpo4777 | Helpee: 好人有好报 | Context: Personal family health crisis | Resolution: Offered prayers and support

## 4. Action Items

Type: Technical | Description: Continue plugin-evm work to unlock more NFT functionality | Mentioned By: Odilitime

Type: Feature | Description: Potential ElizaOK NFT implementation | Mentioned By: Odilitime

Type: Documentation | Description: Clarify relationship between Spartan infrastructure and Milady wallet integration | Mentioned By: Skinny

Type: Documentation | Description: Verify and clarify which token receives the upgrade mentioned in shawmakesmagic tweet | Mentioned By: g
